description: Capable of producing abundant vegetation or offspring; highly productive. 'Muroa' names the quality of generative potential—soil that can grow, beings that can create, conditions that support new life.
sound_symbolism: The word opens to life. 'Mu' begins with mother-earth grounding, the fertile source. Then 'ro' grows with generating force. Finally 'a' opens to possibility, life emerging.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'muroa muila' (fertile earth). Related to 'lorua' (abundant) but emphasizes potential rather than current plenty—fertile ground may not yet be planted.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Fertile soil is the foundation of food sovereignty—regenerative agriculture restores fertility rather than depleting it.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fertility was sacred—the land's ability to produce was honored, protected, and celebrated.
